title: rubus × grantii
intro: Rubus × grantii, also known as the Grant's raspberry or raspberry rose, is a hybrid shrub in the family Rosaceae, resulting from the cross between Rubus idaeus (wild raspberry) and Rubus fruticosus (blackberry). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The health benefits of Rubus × grantii include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, while its herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and jams, are used to support digestive health, alleviate pain, and boost the immune system. In addition, the plant's leaves and stems are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions.
